Who I Am

Product thinker.
Operator at heart.

Personal Mission

To build businesses that move the needle — not just for their shareholders, but for the economies of the countries they operate in.

Especially emerging markets. Especially Africa.

I turn ideas into products people actually want to buy — and businesses actually profit from. Because people don't buy what they need; they buy what they want. My job is making those two things the same.

At RoyalFruity Zobo, I took on the full spectrum of product leadership: from hands-on production floors to designing the go-to-market engine, from building sales funnels to identifying distribution gaps. I learned that great products live or die at the intersection of operations, storytelling, and relentless customer focus.

Long before I had the title, I was doing the work — helping businesses rebrand, coordinating across production, sales, and marketing, simply because I believed in the mission. PM isn't a career pivot. It's what I've always been doing.

→ Doubled production output from 1,200 to 2,400+ bottles/month in under 60 days at RoyalFruity
→ Closed and retained a B2B partnership deal — 200 packs/order for 1+ month, negotiated solo
→ Served as exclusive beverage partner for 2 campus events reaching 3,500+ attendees
→ Scaled Web 79 challenge from 200 to 1,000 users per cycle with a repeatable acquisition engine
→ Founded KickstartCo — guided first cohort of founders from raw idea to execution stage

Founder · 2023–Present
Featured Case Study
RoyalFruity Zobo
Founding Product & Growth Lead · Oct 2023 – Present

How I doubled production, closed a B2B partnership solo, and served 3,500+ people as exclusive beverage partner — all within months of joining an early-stage Nigerian beverage startup.

RoyalFruity Zobo is an early-stage Nigerian beverage startup positioning itself as a premium, experience-driven drink in a crowded market. Joining as a founding team member, I wore every hat that mattered — product, growth, sales, and operations — in a resource-constrained environment where execution quality was the only competitive moat.

Distribution · Events
Exclusive Beverage Partner

Royal Fruity was the exclusive drinks partner for two major campus events — together reaching 3,500+ attendees. Both deals were identified, negotiated, and fulfilled end-to-end.

Product · Operations
From production floor to the pour.
Logistics
Delivery operations at scale
Production
2,400+ bottles/month
⚙️ Operations & Product Execution
Worked directly on production batches, ensuring consistency from formulation to final packaging. Improved workflow across bottling, labeling, and delivery coordination — Scaled production from 1,200 to 2,400+ bottles/month in under 60 days — a 2× output increase through workflow redesign.
📣 Growth & Marketing
Created and executed social content strategies — UGC, reels, and product visuals — that positioned the brand as a premium, experience-driven beverage and fueled organic customer acquisition.
💰 Sales & Revenue Flow
Managed customer outreach and follow-ups. Built trust loops that drove repeat purchases up to 4×/month. Handled invoicing and designed early-stage payment tracking systems from scratch.
🗺️ Strategy & Scaling
Identified growth opportunities across distribution and pricing. Contributed to expansion planning, secured 5+ distribution channels, and fulfilled 3,000+ unit orders for large-scale events.
🤝 Business Development
Assisted in grant sourcing and applications to unlock non-dilutive funding. Supported partnership exploration with 5+ major event organizations, expanding brand reach and revenue channels.
Key domains: Product Strategy GTM Execution Operations Growth Marketing Sales Pipeline Business Dev
Case Study
Web 79
Growth PM · Product Strategy & Growth Systems

How I designed a growth engine for a tech education platform — scaling challenge participants from 200 to 1,000 per cycle with a self-reinforcing acquisition loop.

Web 79 operates in a competitive, low-trust education market where students lack direction in tech skill acquisition, parents are skeptical about ROI, and competitors offer generic, low-outcome training. The core problem wasn't visibility — it was conversion and long-term value creation. I was brought in to build a growth system that didn't just attract learners, but turned them into revenue-generating talent.

Product I helped push to market · Web 79

* Projected metrics based on pilot model · CAC maintained at ₦150 across all stages
🤖 AI Product Line
Launched Machine Learning for Beginners and Agentic AI — Workflow Automation to capture surging demand. These courses generated ~3× higher demand vs traditional offerings, attracted high-intent learners, and unlocked premium pricing potential.
💡 Value Ladder System
Designed a three-tier conversion engine: Entry (low barrier) → Core (monetisation layer) → Backend (revenue expansion). The system improved conversion rates, built an internal talent pipeline, enabled larger client projects, and increased lifetime customer value.
🏆 Tech Wealth Challenge
Built a repeatable acquisition engine structured as Setup → Publicity → Challenge → Retention. Piloted at 200–300 users and scaled to 1,000 users per cycle, creating a strong pipeline into paid programs and dramatically increasing engagement.
📈 Results & Outcomes
~3× increase in course demand · Structured, repeatable acquisition system · Student-to-talent pipeline established · Increased revenue potential · Scalable growth flywheel: Acquisition → Training → Deployment → Revenue → Repeat.
Key domains: Growth Strategy Product Line Design User Acquisition Revenue Systems Talent Pipeline EdTech

Capabilities

What I bring
to the table.

🎯
Product Strategy
Translating market insight and business context into clear product direction. Comfortable with roadmapping, prioritization frameworks, and OKR-style goal setting in early-stage environments.
🚀
Go-to-Market Execution
Designing and running launches end-to-end — from channel selection and messaging to activation and feedback loops. Built GTM systems without formal marketing budgets.
📊
Growth & Analytics
Tracking what matters: customer acquisition, retention rates, and revenue flows. Built early-stage dashboards and sales pipelines to surface actionable insights from raw data.
⚙️
Operations Management
Hands-on experience managing production workflows, delivery coordination, and quality control — bridging the gap between product vision and operational reality.
🤝
Stakeholder Management
Managing relationships across customers, partners, suppliers, and internal teams. Experienced in driving alignment through trust, clear communication, and consistent delivery.
✍️
Content & Brand
Creating compelling product narratives and social content — UGC, reels, product visuals — that build brand equity and drive organic acquisition with limited resources.

Working Together

How I actually
operate.

Not what I know — how I show up.

🔍
I start with the problem, not the solution.

Before I suggest anything, I want to understand what's actually broken — not just what looks broken. I ask uncomfortable questions early so we don't build the wrong thing confidently.

⚡
I move fast and document as I go.

Speed matters in early-stage environments. I bias toward action and course-correct with data. I don't wait for perfect conditions — I build structure while moving.

🤝
I work across the whole team, not just my lane.

I've managed production floors, run sales calls, written content, and built payment systems in the same week. I don't stay in my lane when the mission needs more than that.

📊
I measure what I care about, not everything.

I track the metrics that actually tell me if the product is working — retention, repeat rates, conversion, output. I don't confuse activity with progress.

FAQs

Questions people
usually ask.

The honest answers — because you're probably thinking them anyway.

My training is hands-on. I've built and grown real products — a beverage brand, a tech education platform — from early stage through execution. I hold certifications in Product Management (Aha! PM Professional), Inbound Marketing (HubSpot Academy), AI in Business, and Advanced Lead Generation. But honestly the most useful education has been doing the work and living with the results. Certifications told me the frameworks. Real products taught me when to use them and when to throw them out.

The systems I use to drive results don't change by geography. Structure, detailed market research, and understanding customer behaviour are fundamentals — not local skills. Early-stage environments actually demand more rigour, not less, because you can't hide behind process. I actively follow global market trends and have deliberately built a working style that translates across contexts. I'm fully open to remote, international, and structured corporate environments.

I'm passionate about building businesses from the ground up — specifically ones that create real economic impact. I have a personal mission to help raise businesses that eventually move the needle on the GDP of emerging economies. Product Management is where that vision meets execution. Long before I had the title, I was doing the work — helping an FMCG business rebrand and grow within a university market, coordinating across production, sales, and marketing for free, simply because I believed in the mission. PM isn't a career pivot for me. It's what I've always been doing.

AI-driven automation and product development. I'm learning how to use AI tools to compress timelines — faster prototyping, faster research synthesis, faster iteration. The gap between idea and execution is where most products die, and I think AI closes that gap significantly. I'm building that into how I work.

Completely. Remote, hybrid, international, Lagos-based — I'm open to all of it. What matters most to me is working on a product I believe in, with a team that's serious about building something that lasts.

Got a product
and a market?

Let's map out your go-to-market in 30 minutes. I'm actively looking for a full-time PM role at an ambitious early-stage company — and I take on select consulting engagements for founders who need to move fast.
